narrative.headline: 2018 Volkswagen Atlas: electrical and air bag issues top owner complaints
narrative.summary: Across 452 owner complaints to NHTSA, the electrical system is the most-reported area at 109 reports (24%), followed closely by air bags at 100 reports (22%) and the engine at 96 reports (21%). Most reports did not involve a crash or injury, and no fires or deaths were reported. The air bag and service brake categories each account for 2 of the crashes in this data, and a handful of engine, brake, and collision-avoidance reports mention injuries.

issues.sample: BRAND NEW 2018 ATLAS BATTERY PROBLEM - BATTERY DRAINS COMPLETELY IN LESS THAN 3 DAYS WHEN CAR IS SWITCHED OFF AND PARKED IN ATTACHED GARAGE. HAPPENED TWICE IN TWO MONTHS. SERVICE RESPONDED BY SAYING THERE ARE SEVERAL COMPUTERS THAT DRAIN BATTERY EVEN WHEN CAR IS SHUTDOWN. SUGGEST
issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: Most-reported area: 109 complaints (24%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries reported. Owners describe the battery draining completely after the car sits parked for just a few days.

issues.title: Air Bags
issues.blurb: 100 complaints (22%), including 2 crashes and 2 injuries. Owners describe air bags deploying unexpectedly, in one case nearly all of them firing at once.

issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: 96 complaints (21%), with 3 injuries and no crashes or fires reported. Owners describe excessive oil consumption and check-engine warnings, sometimes within the first few thousand miles.

issues.title: Service Brakes
issues.blurb: 75 complaints (17%), including 2 crashes and 3 injuries. Owners describe the automatic emergency braking suddenly stopping the car when no obstacle was present.

issues.title: Forward Collision Avoidance
issues.blurb: 29 complaints (6%), with 3 injuries and no crashes or fires reported. Owners report problems with the front assist and autonomous emergency systems, sometimes within weeks of ownership.
